item.title:
GOLDMAN WARNS OF POTENTIAL ‘EXTREME’ STOCK RALLY
Goldman Sachs’ John Flood says hedge fund positioning could fuel a sharp stock surge if good news emerges. Speculative bulls have high gross exposure (307%) while heavily shorting ETFs and index futures, creating right-tail upside risk.
A ceasefire in the Iran conflict or positive headlines could trigger 2–3% index gains as macro shorts unwind. Market volatility remains high due to war, AI, credit fears, and thin liquidity, while corporate buybacks support equities. Flood notes stocks need a resolution signal soon or risk further stress.
item.description:
GOLDMAN WARNS OF POTENTIAL ‘EXTREME’ STOCK RALLY

Goldman Sachs’ John Flood says hedge fund positioning could fuel a sharp stock surge if good news emerges. Speculative bulls have high gross exposure (307%) while heavily shorting ETFs and index futures, creating right-tail upside risk.

A ceasefire in the Iran conflict or positive headlines could trigger 2–3% index gains as macro shorts unwind. Market volatility remains high due to war, AI, credit fears, and thin liquidity, while corporate buybacks support equities. Flood notes stocks need a resolution signal soon or risk further stress.

item.title: $TSLA - TESLA SHARES HIT TWO-WEEK HIGH, LAST UP 2.6%; CO'S CHINA-MADE EV SALES JUMP 91% IN FEBRUARY
item.description: $TSLA - TESLA SHARES HIT TWO-WEEK HIGH, LAST UP 2.6%; CO'S CHINA-MADE EV SALES JUMP 91% IN FEBRUARY

item.title:
🚨NO POLICY MOVE CAN STOP CRUDE’S RISE: CARLYLE ENERGY
Jeff Currie, says no policy move can stop crude’s rise, even with a Middle East ceasefire. Supply-chain disruptions—from misplaced ships, canceled insurance, and slowed output in Saudi Arabia, Iraq, and the UAE—will take months to fix. He called oil reserve releases a “minuscule offset” to the ongoing disruption.
item.description:
🚨NO POLICY MOVE CAN STOP CRUDE’S RISE: CARLYLE ENERGY

Jeff Currie, says no policy move can stop crude’s rise, even with a Middle East ceasefire. Supply-chain disruptions—from misplaced ships, canceled insurance, and slowed output in Saudi Arabia, Iraq, and the UAE—will take months to fix. He called oil reserve releases a “minuscule offset” to the ongoing disruption.

item.title: ALL HSBC BRANCHES CLOSED IN QATAR UNTIL FURTHER NOTICE TO ENSURE SAFETY OF CUSTOMERS AND STAFF - TEXT MESSAGE TO CUSTOMERS
item.description: ALL HSBC BRANCHES CLOSED IN QATAR UNTIL FURTHER NOTICE TO ENSURE SAFETY OF CUSTOMERS AND STAFF - TEXT MESSAGE TO CUSTOMERS

item.title:
SLB SUSPENDS MIDDLE EAST OPERATIONS, CUTS Q1 REVENUE OUTLOOK
SLB, the world’s largest oilfield-services provider has halted some operations and employee travel in the Middle East due to regional instability. The company said measures will continue until conditions stabilize and it will resume activity gradually.
SLB warned these disruptions will reduce first-quarter revenue, sending its shares down 2.3% in premarket trading.
item.description:
SLB SUSPENDS MIDDLE EAST OPERATIONS, CUTS Q1 REVENUE OUTLOOK

SLB, the world’s largest oilfield-services provider has halted some operations and employee travel in the Middle East due to regional instability. The company said measures will continue until conditions stabilize and it will resume activity gradually.

SLB warned these disruptions will reduce first-quarter revenue, sending its shares down 2.3% in premarket trading.

item.title:
HORMUZ TRAFFIC RISES AS IRAN-LINKED SHIPS MOVE
Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz remains mostly suspended, but vessel-tracking data shows a slight uptick in Iran- and China-linked traffic, including two sanctioned VLCCs fully loaded with crude.
Eight commercial transits were recorded Tuesday, with four more early Wednesday. Recent incidents, including the Mayuree Naree being hit and another vessel turning back, highlight ongoing missile, drone, and security risks.
Since Feb. 28, 13.7 million barrels of Iranian oil have passed the strait, though many ships avoid AIS tracking, making full monitoring difficult. Most commercial tonnage remains blocked until maritime security improves.
item.description:
HORMUZ TRAFFIC RISES AS IRAN-LINKED SHIPS MOVE

Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz remains mostly suspended, but vessel-tracking data shows a slight uptick in Iran- and China-linked traffic, including two sanctioned VLCCs fully loaded with crude.

Eight commercial transits were recorded Tuesday, with four more early Wednesday. Recent incidents, including the Mayuree Naree being hit and another vessel turning back, highlight ongoing missile, drone, and security risks.

Since Feb. 28, 13.7 million barrels of Iranian oil have passed the strait, though many ships avoid AIS tracking, making full monitoring difficult. Most commercial tonnage remains blocked until maritime security improves.

item.title: 🇯🇵 JAPAN INDUSTRY MINISTRY SAYS PLAN TO RELEASE OIL FROM NATIONAL RESERVES FROM AROUND LATE MARCH
item.description: 🇯🇵 JAPAN INDUSTRY MINISTRY SAYS PLAN TO RELEASE OIL FROM NATIONAL RESERVES FROM AROUND LATE MARCH

item.title:
IRAN’S SHAHED DRONE PRODUCTION SLOWS BUT STOCKPILES REMAIN
Iran’s production of Shahed-136 drone has slowed due to U.S. and Israeli strikes, but existing stockpiles remain and new drones can be made using simple components. Over 2,100 Shaheds have been fired, damaging oil infrastructure, airports, and military equipment.
While Israel’s defenses have intercepted many, the drones’ sheer numbers strain interceptor supplies. Shaheds are easy to launch with minimal infrastructure, unlike ballistic missiles, whose production is now nearly halted. The Strait of Hormuz remains a key target for Iranian attacks, including drones and mines, threatening global oil flows.
item.description:
IRAN’S SHAHED DRONE PRODUCTION SLOWS BUT STOCKPILES REMAIN

Iran’s production of Shahed-136 drone has slowed due to U.S. and Israeli strikes, but existing stockpiles remain and new drones can be made using simple components. Over 2,100 Shaheds have been fired, damaging oil infrastructure, airports, and military equipment.

While Israel’s defenses have intercepted many, the drones’ sheer numbers strain interceptor supplies. Shaheds are easy to launch with minimal infrastructure, unlike ballistic missiles, whose production is now nearly halted. The Strait of Hormuz remains a key target for Iranian attacks, including drones and mines, threatening global oil flows.

item.title: JUST IN: Israeli intelligence assessment indicates Iran’s new supreme leader was wounded at the start of the war
item.description: JUST IN: Israeli intelligence assessment indicates Iran’s new supreme leader was wounded at the start of the war

item.title:
IRAN ATTACKS TWO SHIPS IN STRAIT OF HORMUZ
Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said it attacked two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz after they ignored warnings.
The Thai-flagged bulk carrier Mayuree Naree and Liberian-flagged Express Rome were struck by Iranian fire, according to Fars News Agency.
The UK Maritime Trade Operations says 13 vessel attacks have occurred in the region since the conflict escalated on Feb. 28, including three today. Iran warned ships linked to the U.S. or Israel could be targeted.
item.description:
IRAN ATTACKS TWO SHIPS IN STRAIT OF HORMUZ

Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said it attacked two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz after they ignored warnings.

The Thai-flagged bulk carrier Mayuree Naree and Liberian-flagged Express Rome were struck by Iranian fire, according to Fars News Agency.

The UK Maritime Trade Operations says 13 vessel attacks have occurred in the region since the conflict escalated on Feb. 28, including three today. Iran warned ships linked to the U.S. or Israel could be targeted.

item.title:
Pinned: RECESSION ODDS FALL TO 28% AS EXPANSION HOLDS
Kalshi traders now see a 28% chance of a U.S. recession in 2026, down from recent highs of 34–36%. Wells Fargo says the expansion should continue, supported by services, demographics, and fiscal policy. A major, persistent oil shock would be needed to trigger a recession. https://kalshi.com/markets/kxrecssnber/recession/kxrecssnber-26?utm_source=walterbloomberg
item.description:
RECESSION ODDS FALL TO 28% AS EXPANSION HOLDS

Kalshi traders now see a 28% chance of a U.S. recession in 2026, down from recent highs of 34–36%. Wells Fargo says the expansion should continue, supported by services, demographics, and fiscal policy. A major, persistent oil shock would be needed to trigger a recession.
kalshi.com/markets/kxrecssnb…

item.title:
ISRAEL SEES NO GUARANTEE OF IRANIAN UPRISING
Israel says there is no certainty Iran’s government will collapse despite the U.S.-led bombing campaign, a senior official told Reuters. Civilians remain fearful, and no uprising has emerged, even after the deaths of leaders including Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
Israeli leaders emphasize they cannot force regime change, with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu saying it is ultimately up to Iranians. The U.S. and Israel have not set clear public conditions for ending the conflict, while President Donald Trump says the war will continue until objectives are met.
item.description:
ISRAEL SEES NO GUARANTEE OF IRANIAN UPRISING

Israel says there is no certainty Iran’s government will collapse despite the U.S.-led bombing campaign, a senior official told Reuters. Civilians remain fearful, and no uprising has emerged, even after the deaths of leaders including Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

Israeli leaders emphasize they cannot force regime change, with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu saying it is ultimately up to Iranians. The U.S. and Israel have not set clear public conditions for ending the conflict, while President Donald Trump says the war will continue until objectives are met.

item.title: SPOT SILVER FALLS OVER 3% TO $85.33/OZ
item.description: SPOT SILVER FALLS OVER 3% TO $85.33/OZ

item.title: DIPLOMATIC ADVISOR TO UAE'S PRESIDENT: IRANIANS LYING WHEN THEY CLAIM ONLY ATTACKING U.S. BASES IN THE GULF, THEIR ATTACKS HIT CIVILIAN INFRASTRUCTURE
item.description: DIPLOMATIC ADVISOR TO UAE'S PRESIDENT: IRANIANS LYING WHEN THEY CLAIM ONLY ATTACKING U.S. BASES IN THE GULF, THEIR ATTACKS HIT CIVILIAN INFRASTRUCTURE

item.title:
IRAN CONFLICT SHIFTS FED RATE CUT EXPECTATIONS
The Iran conflict and rising oil prices are reshaping expectations for U.S. monetary policy. Morgan Stanley says the Fed is now more likely to delay cuts or cut deeper than previously forecast.
Chief U.S. economist Michael Gapen notes inflation at 3% and low unemployment could push cuts to later in 2026, possibly three reductions lowering rates to 2.75–3%. The baseline still expects two cuts in June and September, but risks now favor later, potentially larger easing.
item.description:
IRAN CONFLICT SHIFTS FED RATE CUT EXPECTATIONS

The Iran conflict and rising oil prices are reshaping expectations for U.S. monetary policy. Morgan Stanley says the Fed is now more likely to delay cuts or cut deeper than previously forecast.

Chief U.S. economist Michael Gapen notes inflation at 3% and low unemployment could push cuts to later in 2026, possibly three reductions lowering rates to 2.75–3%. The baseline still expects two cuts in June and September, but risks now favor later, potentially larger easing.

item.title:
TRUMP WELCOMES IRAN TO U.S. WORLD CUP
President Donald Trump told FIFA that Iran is welcome to play in the World Cup in the U.S., officials say. The discussion with FIFA president Gianni Infantino and White House task force director Andrew Giuliani touched on the ongoing war, now in its 12th day. Infantino later wrote that the World Cup is needed “to bring people together now more than ever.”
item.description:
TRUMP WELCOMES IRAN TO U.S. WORLD CUP

President Donald Trump told FIFA that Iran is welcome to play in the World Cup in the U.S., officials say. The discussion with FIFA president Gianni Infantino and White House task force director Andrew Giuliani touched on the ongoing war, now in its 12th day. Infantino later wrote that the World Cup is needed “to bring people together now more than ever.”

IRAN THREATENS OIL BLOCKADE IN STRAIT OF HORMUZ
“We will not allow even a single liter of oil to pass through the Strait of Hormuz for the benefit of the US, the Zionist regime or their partners.”

item.title:
GET CAUGHT UP
🔸International Energy Agency may propose releasing 300–400 million barrels from reserves.
🔸Brent crude still climbed above $90 despite the potential release.
🔸G7 ministers will meet later to discuss a coordinated stockpile release.
🔸Markets turned volatile after mixed signals from U.S. officials.
🔸Military strikes continue across the Middle East.
🔸Three ships were hit, and drones fell near Dubai Airport.
item.description:
GET CAUGHT UP

🔸International Energy Agency may propose releasing 300–400 million barrels from reserves.

🔸Brent crude still climbed above $90 despite the potential release.

🔸G7 ministers will meet later to discuss a coordinated stockpile release.

🔸Markets turned volatile after mixed signals from U.S. officials.

🔸Military strikes continue across the Middle East.

🔸Three ships were hit, and drones fell near Dubai Airport.

item.description: BINANCE - FILES LAWSUIT AGAINST WALL STREET JOURNAL OVER REPORTING

item.title:
🚨 IRAN SAYS OIL WILL REACH $200 A BARREL, WARNS OF 'CONTINUOUS STRIKES'
Iran will switch from "reciprocal hits" after attacks to continuous strikes on adversaries, and the U.S. will not be able to control oil prices, the spokesperson for Tehran's Khatam al-Anbiya military command headquarters said on Wednesday.
"We won't allow even one litre of oil to reach the U.S., Zionists (Israel) and their partners. Any vessel or tanker bound to them will be a legitimate target," Ebrahim Zolfaqari said.
"Get ready for the oil barrel to be at $200 because the oil price depends on the regional security which you have destabilised," he added
item.description:
🚨 IRAN SAYS OIL WILL REACH $200 A BARREL, WARNS OF 'CONTINUOUS STRIKES'

Iran will switch from "reciprocal hits" after attacks to continuous strikes on adversaries, and the U.S. will not be able to control oil prices, the spokesperson for Tehran's Khatam al-Anbiya military command headquarters said on Wednesday.

"We won't allow even one litre of oil to reach the U.S., Zionists (Israel) and their partners. Any vessel or tanker bound to them will be a legitimate target," Ebrahim Zolfaqari said.

"Get ready for the oil barrel to be at $200 because the oil price depends on the regional security which you have destabilised," he added
